gauja.description: Latvia's most popular river — stunning sandstone cliffs, ancient caves, and pristine forests. The Gauja Valley is a national park and one of the most scenic landscapes in the Baltics. Perfect for all skill levels with a gentle current and plenty of stopping points.

highlights.name: Sandstone cliffs
highlights.desc: Dramatic 20m+ formations carved by the river over thousands of years — some of the tallest in the Baltics.

highlights.name: Gutmanis Cave
highlights.desc: Latvia's largest cave with historic inscriptions dating back to the 17th century. Accessible directly from the water.

highlights.name: Turaida Castle
highlights.desc: A 13th-century red brick castle with panoramic views over the Gauja valley — a highlight of any trip near Sigulda.

salaca.description: Wild and scenic — crystal-clear waters, dramatic red sandstone outcrops, and exciting rapids. One of the cleanest rivers in Latvia and a favourite for paddlers looking for adventure. The upper sections offer Class I-II rapids while the lower stretches are more relaxed.

highlights.name: Red sandstone cliffs
highlights.desc: Striking terracotta-coloured rock formations rising directly from the water's edge — unlike anything on the Gauja.

highlights.name: Crystal-clear water
highlights.desc: The Salaca is one of the clearest rivers in Latvia — you can see the riverbed at several metres depth.

highlights.name: Class I–II rapids
highlights.desc: Exciting but manageable rapids in the upper sections add a sense of adventure suitable for most paddlers.

brasla.description: A fast-flowing tributary of the Gauja with exciting rapids and beautiful gorge scenery. Best paddled in spring when water levels are at their highest. Shorter routes make it ideal for a half-day or full-day adventure.

highlights.name: Spring rapids
highlights.desc: High spring water levels create the most exciting rapids on any Latvian river — best experienced April through June.

highlights.name: Scenic gorge
highlights.desc: The Brasla cuts through a narrow forested gorge with steep banks and dramatic overhanging vegetation.

highlights.name: Gauja confluence
highlights.desc: End your paddle where the Brasla meets the Gauja — a satisfying and scenic finish to any route.

amata.description: A hidden gem among Gauja's tributaries — the Amata cuts through deep forest valleys with dramatic rock outcrops and crystal-clear water. The famous Zvārtes rock is one of Latvia's most spectacular natural landmarks.

highlights.name: Zvārtes Rock
highlights.desc: Latvia's most dramatic riverside rock formation — a 15m sandstone outcrop that towers above the water. Unmissable.

highlights.name: Deep forest valleys
highlights.desc: The Amata flows through some of the most untouched forest in Vidzeme — completely secluded from roads and settlements.

highlights.name: Crystal-clear water
highlights.desc: Fed by natural springs, the Amata has exceptional water clarity — cool, clean, and perfect for swimming stops.
